intro: Tapinanthus kayseri, also known as the yellow mistletoe, is a parasitic plant species that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the vascular tissues of its host tree, typically through the roots or stems. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of T. kayseri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
